current_judgment: Schema updated (2026-03-21): frozen dimension set expanded from 5 to 6. voice_consistency narrowed to tonal/register/stylistic surface stability only. pov_fidelity added as dedicated dimension for perspective contract enforcement. POV validation post-split (qwen2.5:14b): scene-07 (omniscient slip) passes pov_fidelity ≤ 0.65 at 0.60; scene-08 (clean POV) passes ≥ 0.80 at 1.00. scene-04 (first-person intrusion) and scene-06 (reader address) score pov_fidelity 1.00 — model does not penalize POV violations at the score level regardless of which dimension key is used. The split is the correct schema design and cleanly separates tonal from perspective concerns for consumers, but qwen2.5:14b cannot reliably produce low pov_fidelity scores for hard POV failures. This is the same model capability limit documented in round-2. pov_fidelity scores from this model should not be used as gate signals for POV enforcement.
next_required_decision: Decide whether to pursue candidate_baseline with pov_fidelity documented as unreliable for qwen2.5:14b (scores advisory only, not gate-eligible), or evaluate a more capable model against the updated 6-dimension schema before baseline promotion. If proceeding with qwen2.5:14b: run full 5-scene baseline set against the updated prompt to confirm no regressions on non-POV dimensions, then promote to candidate_baseline with pov_fidelity limitation documented in provenance_notes. If evaluating a new model: test against all 4 POV scenes with pov_fidelity thresholds as the primary gate criteria.
provenance_notes: Metrics derived from operator judgment across 5 eval scenes (run-2026-03-21-001 through run-2026-03-21-005). schema_reliability: 5/5 runs returned valid schema envelope. false_positive_rate: 0 clearly incorrect findings identified across all 5 runs. surface_detection_rate: 4/4 targeted style issues detected (scene-04 counted as detected despite dimension miscategorization). All metrics are operator-judged from a small 5-scene set. Not benchmark-derived. Not gate-eligible. metric_profile is set to detection_reasoning as the closest available value; style analysis identifies issues and reasons about them but is not a binary detection task.
